name: primekg
tagline: Local Python/CSV skill for querying the PrimeKG precision-medicine knowledge graph (genes, drugs, diseases, phenotypes).
verdict: No security issues: purely local, read-only CSV queries with no network egress, secrets access, or hidden instructions. Main risk is functional, not security: the data path is hardcoded to the original author's personal machine (C:\Users\eamon\...), inconsistently duplicated as a WSL mount path, and no guidance covers a missing-data or no-match case, so it m

highlights.summary: No 'do NOT use when' guidance is given, so a user could invoke it for biology questions outside PrimeKG's actual coverage (e.g. entities absent from the graph).

Data path is given twice in inconsistent forms: 'C:\Users\eamon\Documents\Data\PrimeKG\kg.csv' in Overview vs '/mnt/c/Users/eamon/Documents/Data/PrimeKG/kg.csv' in Resources; node counts also drift ('
No handling is described for a missing data file, an unmatched search, or ambiguous entity names — a likely failure path given the hardcoded personal path.
